mnemonics: C-BASS: Constructors Build Ancestors (Superclass) First, Then Self. Remember the order: Constructor calls travel up the inheritance chain before building the current object.
keyPoints: A constructor for a superclass is always executed before a constructor for its subclass.
If a constructor does not explicitly call a superclass constructor, the compiler automatically inserts a call to the superclass's no-argument constructor.
If a superclass does not have a no-argument constructor, the subclass constructor must explicitly call one of the superclass's constructors using the 'super' keyword.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that the compiler automatically calls super() if no other constructor call is present, leading to errors if the superclass lacks a no-argument constructor.
Assuming constructors are executed in the order they are written in the class, rather than from the top of the inheritance hierarchy down.
